text.title: strategy-methods Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for strategy-methods as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Criteria that partially overlap (e.g., Cost, Time to value, and Risk all encoding capital exposure).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Double-counting inflates one dimension and makes the matrix systematically biased toward options that score well on capital risk, even when that was not the intent.

failure-mode.description: Weighted-score laundering — weights drift to make the math agree with the pre-decided recommendation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without weights locked before options are scored (in a separate weight-setting workshop documented), the score is decorative.

failure-mode.description: Five-point-scale clustering — every option scores 3 or 4 on every criterion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force anchored definitions or switch to {-1, 0, +1} pairwise vs. baseline.

failure-mode.description: Missing baseline / do-nothing column — recommendation reads as advocacy without status-quo option.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: README explicitly says "include do nothing baseline" — agents skip it when rushed.

failure-mode.description: No sensitivity analysis — reasonable ±10% weight wobble flips the winner.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always recompute the ranking with weights at ±10% and ±25% before publishing.

failure-mode.description: Criterion overlap — "Cost", "Time to value", and "Risk" all partly encode the same capital exposure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run a correlation check or merge criteria.

failure-mode.description: Ordinal × cardinal confusion — weights are percentages but scores are ordinal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The output is rank-ordering at best; call it a ranking aid, not a value.

failure-mode.description: Limitation register without root-cause — defects are listed; root causes are guessed; remediation lands on symptoms.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: README mandates the root-cause column — agents fill it with restated symptoms unless explicitly forced to use 5-whys / fishbone sub-routine.

failure-mode.description: Severity inflation — without a severity rubric, every limitation is "High" within a week.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prioritization breaks when all items are marked high.

failure-mode.description: 50-technique table as menu, not method — agents pick a technique by name match without checking applicability.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The table is a router; once selected, read the BABOK section for context.

failure-mode.description: Static weights across stakeholders — finance, ops, and engineering rationally weight criteria differently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A multi-stakeholder weight matrix surfaces tensions that a single vector hides.
